user_message: Working on Woodgrove Labs's territory planning. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 13:16 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs territory planning

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 08:30 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Woodgrove Labs

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 12:32 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Woodgrove Labs

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-I trap (Skips whitespace context -- proposes redundant motion); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-I failure mode (Skips whitespace context -- proposes redundant motion)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-I -- Skips whitespace context -- proposes redundant motion
